---
title: "hjkl label-pan + configurable arrow passthrough"
author: "Yun Jiang"
format:
  html:
    html-math-method: mathjax
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <link rel="icon" href="data:image/svg+xml,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><text y='.9em' font-size='90'>⌨️</text></svg>">
---

# Plan

## Objective

Free the extended-cluster arrow keys while the overlay is up so they reach the
**app beneath** (Excel cell nav, list focus, editor movement), and move
label-panning to Vim-style **`hjkl`** chords. A new hot-reload `ArrowKeyBehavior`
setting keeps the legacy arrow-panning available for anyone who wants it.

## Why

In continuous mode the overlay currently steals plain arrows for panning
(`src/HuntAndPeck/Services/OverlayKeyboardHook.cs:232-238`), but arrows are
more often wanted by the app beneath. `hjkl` is preferred over any
arrow+modifier scheme because `Shift+Arrow`, `Ctrl+Arrow`, and `Ctrl+Shift+Arrow`
are high-demand app shortcuts (selection, word-jump); `Shift+hjkl` /
`Ctrl+Shift+hjkl` collide with almost nothing.

## Locked design

- Plain extended arrows → **passthrough by default** (`ArrowKeyBehavior=Passthrough`);
  `ArrowKeyBehavior=Pan` restores legacy arrow-panning. Hot-reload.
- **`Shift+hjkl` = large step** (15px, reuses `NudgeStepFast`).
- **`Ctrl+Shift+hjkl` = small step** (3px, reuses `NudgeStep`).
- Vim mapping: `h`=←, `j`=↓, `k`=↑, `l`=→.
- `Ctrl+C` / `Ctrl+V` / all Ctrl-shortcuts stay untouched: the new pan capture
  requires `Shift` held AND key ∈ {H,J,K,L}; anything without Shift, or Shift
  with another key, is unchanged (`ctrl||win` → `None` → passthrough).

## Scope / files

1. `src/HuntAndPeck/Services/OverlayActionConfig.cs` — `ArrowKeyBehavior` enum,
   `ParseArrowKeyBehavior`, `ReadArrowKeyBehavior` (default `Passthrough`).
2. `src/NativeMethods/User32.cs` — named `VK_H/J/K/L` constants.
3. `src/HuntAndPeck/Services/OverlayKeyboardHook.cs` — split `ctrl`/`win` in
   `KeyboardProc`; read `arrowPan` per keydown; collapse the two `Classify`
   overloads into one optional-param method; add hjkl pan branch; gate the
   extended-arrow nudge on `arrowPan`.
4. `src/HuntAndPeck/App.config` — `ArrowKeyBehavior` setting + refreshed
   `NudgeStep`/`NudgeStepFast` comments.
5. `src/HuntAndPeck/ViewModels/OptionsViewModel.cs` + `Views/OptionsView.xaml`
   — expose `ArrowKeyBehavior` (ComboBox Passthrough/Pan) + refreshed labels.
6. `src/HuntAndPeck.Tests/Services/OverlayKeyboardHookTest.cs` — new xUnit cases.
7. `CLAUDE.md` — runtime + config doc updates.

No new `.cs` files → no `.csproj` `<Compile Include>` edits.

## Risks

- `Shift+hjkl` previously typed an uppercase hint char; now pans. Plain `hjkl`
  still matches the same label (case-insensitive match,
  `OverlayViewModel.cs:323`), so no hint-typing is lost.
- `arrowPan` is read per keydown via `EnsureFresh` (stat-based, cheap);
  keydowns are low-frequency.
- `Ctrl+Shift+hjkl` does not collide with global hotkeys `Ctrl+Shift+M` /
  `Ctrl+Shift+,`.

## Verification

1. CI gate (`.github/workflows/build.yml`, windows-latest): MSBuild compiles +
   xUnit green (existing + new). Linux box cannot build/run — CI is the gate.
2. `ship-drop` to the Windows box, then manual: arrows move app focus;
   `Shift+hjkl` pans ~15px; `Ctrl+Shift+hjkl` ~3px; `Ctrl+C/V/Z` still work;
   plain `hjkl` still matches labels; `Win+Shift+hjkl` reaches the OS;
   `ArrowKeyBehavior=Pan` restores legacy.
3. Hot-reload: toggle in Options, re-trigger without restart.

See report: `20260723-194232_hjkl-pan-arrow-passthrough.report.qmd`.
